channel.title: Blogs on Mez Says
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Mez Says

item.title: Store This
item.description: Hello and Happy New Year! What did you get up to for New Year’s? Lockdown you say? Well let’s blow all that away and get reading shall we? This post is about an Android library from Dropbox called Store (Store 4 to be precise). It facilitates the repository pattern, has been rebuilt from the ground up using Kotlin Coroutines, and is a great way to accelerate your app to becoming reactive. I’ve been using it for about a year in production during their alpha phase and now that it has recently gone stable I want to share a post of how we use it.

item.title: Izzy Wizzy Lets Get Bezier
item.description:
This week I’ve been focussed on improving the biometric setup journey for the app that I work on. I’ve taken the opportunity to add a little bit of fun / delight into the setup process and learn more about Animated Vector Drawables in Android.

This post aims to recap what I learned when working with Animated Vector Drawables (AVDs).

item.title: How I Like To Test
item.description: I have enjoyed testing as much as I have enjoyed developing. Sometimes more. Sometimes less. Just as it is immensely satisfying when you watch something you have built run, or be used, by a user it is also immensely satisfying to watch those little green ticks appear in your test reports. Here’s how I like to test with a focus on Android.

item.title: Welcome Back
item.description: I’m back! With a slight redesign of the blog. Can you tell?

item.title: Kotcha
item.description: Well it has been a long time between posts but I promise it is for a good reason. I have been busy learning. Learning Kotlin. Kotlin is a replacement for Java and has gained massive popularity on the Android platform. It is compatible with Java down to the bytecode level meaning you can have a mixed project - some Java some Kotlin. Whilst this sounds like it would be a great way to slowly migrate a large project this comes with some gotchas.

item.title: Postcards From The Edge
item.description: After a year long read I’ve finally finished Postcards From The Edge by Carrie Fisher.

item.title: Christmas In Ireland
item.description: I usually flip flop between Ireland and the U.K. for Christmas holidays. This year is Ireland’s turn.

item.title: Droidcon London 2017
item.description: A little late but here is my write up on Droidcon London 2017!

item.title: New Blog Design
item.description: Hello! I’ve been away a while but I’m back!

item.title: Making A Scene
item.description:
For the past few weeks I’ve been taking a brief detour into the Transitions framework in Android. I’ve building a success screen that initially shows a full screen image and text and later transitions into a summary of your transaction.

This post documents some of the things I’ve learned whilst working on this.

item.title: Under The Hood Optimisations
item.description: I’ve spent the last few weeks wrestling with Ruby and making some site changes to my blog. It’s been enjoyable. I’ve learned more about Ruby and more about responsive images on the web. I still don’t like Ruby, but at least now I have made my peace with it.

item.title: Snacking
item.description: A week after coming back from snowboarding I felt a little nostalgic for all the yummy food I had been missing.

item.title: Snowboarding Meribel
item.description: Last week I went to Méribel in France for some snowboarding with friends.

item.title: My Review Of The Film Silence
item.description: <!-- raw HTML omitted --> <!-- raw HTML omitted -->

item.title: End Of The Year
item.description: It’s the end of the year and I fancy writing something about it! I had planned on writing about all the things that happened during the year. However I probably should have blogged more if that were the case. Instead I will focus on why I left my old job in the Autumn.

item.title: Carrie
Beep Beep Boop I Am A Robot
item.description: I’ve been watching two programmes recently about robots and specifically about the artificial intelligence of robots: Humans on Channel 4 and Westworld on HBO.

item.title: Ups And Downs
item.description: I’ve had a lot of ups and downs over the past few months…….. I joined a trampolining centre!

item.title: Deconstructing Tweets
item.description:
As part of my personal project for Spring, and to coincide with the UK 2015 General Election, I decided to create some bots that mirrored the main party candidates and translated their tweets to Jive. They are still running so you can find them on Twitter: Ed Jiveaband, Nick Jivegg, David Jiveron.

One of the more interesting problems that I faced was how to prevent special Tweet Entities being erroneously translated by the Jive translator. You may be asking what is a Tweet Entity? Let me explain about them first and then go on to explain how I can tackle such a problem.

item.title: The Jive Election 2015
item.description: Throughout March and April of 2015 I have been busy working on a pet project with my friend Ricky Shelton. The idea was simple. Make the politicians running in the 2015 UK General Election more electable by having them speak Jive.

item.title: Bathroom All Done
item.description:
At last!! Almost…………

This week we put the final touches to the bathroom remodel. Here’s the final (almost final) bathroom.

item.title: Roasted Spicy Chicken With Rice
item.description: I spotted this recipe for spicy chicken wings on Google+ and thought I would give it a go. I wanted to put my own spin on it which is why I opted for chicken legs instead of wings and modified some of the ingredients. I also didn’t want to buy thirty chicken wings! The recipe does use ketchup but bear with me, it’s not that bad.

item.title: Shopping for a bathroom
item.description: One of the exciting events to happen this year is that we are buying our first house. We are moving just down the road in Bristol to a three bedroom terraced house. The house itself is a Victorian house that has been recently renovated so needs minimal work once we move in.

item.title: Watching TV
item.description: I have become very addicted to streaming video and TV series in particular. Netflix has been my go to choice for this. Although I also still enjoy traditional methods.

item.title: Hello 2015!!
item.description:
Hello 2015!!

I started off last year with a blog post and then got side tracked. And then throughout the year I got side tracked some more. And then, well……… I sort of was on a roll with getting side tracked and decided to not post any recipes, geeky updates, random blog thoughts for the entire year. Well we started a new year this week and I’d like to make amends!

item.title: Catha's Seat Design Ideas
item.description: A good friend of mine, Nick, is organising the construction of a bicycle seat on route 18 of the Cycle Network to complete the work started by his mother, Catha. You can read more about it on the Catha’s Seat website.

item.title: Slow Cooked Beef Ragu
item.description: Set this up in the morning / afternoon and it will be lovely just in time for dinner! Adapted from this BBC Good Food recipe.

item.title: Chilli Garlic Olive Oil
item.description: Inspired by our Italian friend Fran, I’m going to share a nice way to restock your olive oil. It is just regular olive oil but with a kick of chilli! Goes great over pasta and fish but you can use it where ever you would use olive oil. It is also very easy to make. The longer you leave it in the cupboard the better it gets.

item.title: Currys And Why The High Street Should Die
item.description: I recently bought a new camera to play with (Fuji X-E1) from the long standing high street retailer in the UK, Currys. I bought the accessories (a camera bag, a lens, memory cards, and a spare battery) from the online retailer Amazon. I vowed never to use one of them again. Ever.

item.title: Gits Everywhere
item.description: Another long break between blog posts. Mostly out of laziness but also because I am trying to learn and teach myself Android programming. Here’s a short post about how I’m getting along.

item.title: Coffee Cake
item.description: Back on the cake making! This time with a sugar filled coffee cake that is easy peasy to make. I would leave this for special occasions since the ingredients are rather rich and on the excessive side. Makes for a great cake!

item.title: Minty lamb roast
item.description: I used to cook something similar to this whilst at university. It lasts at least two meals even with these measures and there is enough left over for a tasty sandwich too! It was so tasty, in fact, that I used to finish most of it in one. That’s probably why went up a trouser size!

item.title: Lemon Drizzle Cake
item.description: Lemon drizzle cake is something that I have wanted to bake for a while but have never got around to doing. I’m glad that I gave it a go because it is one of the simplest things to make and very tasty!

item.title: Mozzarella bruschetta with courgette
item.description: Here’s a nice, filling and tasty starter that could easily substitute as a main. A different take on the tomato bruschetta starter. We modified this from a BBC recipe that we cooked for Valentine’s Day. It’s veggie friendly too!!

item.title: Andorra snow
item.description: This week I’m with my girlfriend and her best friend for some snow fun in Andorra. Half way through the trip and I can say with confidence that we are loving it! I have another trip with friends from work later on in March so wanted to use this trip to perfect my technique on the snowboard. I also wanted to get my girlfriend interested in snow holidays since they are the highlight of my year and I would love if she came to more of them with me.

item.title: Mushroom Soup
item.description: Of late, I have been subscribed to an interesting food channel on YouTube called RecipeWars. The concept is simple: two chefs head to head, best recipe wins. Only they don’t really win anything it is for funsies. What you do get is two enthusiastic chefs trying out two recipes (largely from famous US chefs) whilst providing some rather good commentary about the ingredients and what works well. Whilst I would prefer a slightly broader scope in recipe sources - such as those from international chefs - I have enjoyed watching the channel and continue to do so.

item.title: Mass Renaming & Restoring USB Drives
item.description: I recently had to rename a lot of files in directory as well as fix a broken USB drive. I reckon I’ll need to use this again at some point so here are the tips. Both for me and for you!

item.title: Kruger Park
item.description:
Kruger Park South Africa

The day starts early since we have a seven hour drive to Kruger from Johannesburg. For the first part I mostly sleep whilst drooling over Anne-Marie because my mouth was hanging open.

item.title: Three Days South Africa
item.description: Week two and I’ll just keep things brief. As we move through the Western Cape into the Eastern and then up into the Northern Cape the Internet availability at hotels and restaurants gets sparser and sparser.

item.title: South Africa Days Four To Six
item.description:
South Africa Day Four

Writing in the style of Dr Seuss was difficult and challenging. I don’t think I’ll do that again. Instead I’ll try a telegram.

Table Top mountain from the north side STOP Quick coffee on the beach STOP Penguin colony at Betty’s Bay STOP Sean met his cousins STOP Got on famously STOP

item.title: Cape Of Good Hope And Kirstenbosch Gardens
item.description:
South Africa Day Three

My last blog post aimed to be in the style of American Psycho. Reading it back to myself it sounds more like Alan Partridge. So I will try something different this time. I will write this on the style of Dr Seuss.

item.title: Table Top And Wine Tasting
item.description:
South Africa Day One - Two

We’re on holiday in South Africa for two weeks during which I’ll try and write a few blog posts about the experience in the style of Patrick Bateman from Bret Easton Ellis’ American Psycho.

item.title: Countdown to South Africa
item.description: Only a few weeks to go until we travel to South Africa for a two week holiday and nothing but sun!

item.title: Books And Cheese
item.description: This weekend I completed two activities. The first was finish reading Frankenstein and the second was to finish a wheel of cheese from Cheddar.

item.title: Gym & Food
item.description: This past month I’ve been trying out a new exercise routine. It involves high intensity interval training and more weights than I would normally do. The idea is to stimulate my body to have a higher proportion of lean muscle than fat. So far it has worked well! Muscle seems to burn off a lot more excess weight whilst I’m not at the gym than long distance running that I used to do.

item.title: The Connections Game
item.description: Played this about a month ago with my girlfriend. Good fun, especially with beer!

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S3
item.description: Here are my thoughts on the Samsung Galaxy S3 after about a months worth of use.

item.title: The end is in sight
item.description: Here’s what’s been happening this past month.

item.title: Porting nightmare
item.description: Here in the UK we are allowed to port our mobile phone number to another provider when we leave. This means that you can keep the same number but switch contracts to a different provider. However you can’t do this from within the same provider.

item.title: Wedding day
item.description:
Today is wedding day.

Now, I don’t know if you’ve been to a wedding where you don’t know anyone but it is quite fun. You can people watch to your heart’s content and no one says anything.

item.title: I'm off
item.description: Right, I’m off to Ireland for a wedding and a five year old’s birthday. Still more excited about the birthday party than the wedding since I don’t know anyone at the wedding and there will be a BBQ at the party. Who can win over a BBQ? Exactly!

item.title: Five year olds and weddings
item.description: Next week I’m off to Ireland for a few days to attend a wedding. I thought I had a suitable suit to wear but this turned out not to be the case. I merely had a black pair of shoes. I don’t think I even have suitable socks. Unless I try out the Jacko look and go with white socks and black shoes? Somehow I don’t think my girlfriend would be too impressed. So this weekend I was ordered to buy a suit.

item.title: In preparation for South Africa
item.description: Come Autumn time, we will be taking a trip for two weeks around South Africa. This will be the first time I have visited South Africa as well as Africa itself. I have a few friends from South Africa who always rave about it and many others who have visited there and have recommended holidays there. This year I’m going to see it for myself!

item.title: Jubilee
item.description: This weekend is the diamond jubilee for the Queen. Here’s a selection of pictures from the day.

item.title: Sean Visits Bristol Zoo
item.description: This weekend we took our favourite little penguin, Sean, to Bristol Zoo. It was incredibly hot this weekend in the U.K. so it was a good chance to get out and about. I think Sean liked it too!

item.title: Fun Bank Holiday in Bristol
item.description: For the May Bank holiday, we spent the time in Bristol.

item.title: Dublin trip
item.description: Took the train from Foxford to Dublin on Friday morning which took three and a half hours but meant that we avoided the insanity of the Titanic celebrations in Lahardane.

item.title: Easter in Ireland
item.description: Easter has begun Ireland with the eating of chocolate eggs.

item.title: Snowboarding Tignes
item.description: This week I’m in Tignes, France snowboarding with some friends from work.

item.title: Hmmmm Steak
item.description: Yesterday I was hungry so I chanced my arm at cooking a steak. I’ve never tried cooking a steak before and didn’t really know what I was doing when I started but here is what I did. For record, I followed the cooking suggestions on the back of the steak packet and didn’t just wing it!

item.title: Defend The Castle! Say NO to the demolition! Now in easy cut and paste format!
item.description: Hi everyone! You can use this to easily draft your objections to the planned demolition of the greatest pub in Battersea, The Castle! You can find this information and more on the Save The Castle website as well as on flyers and cards in The Castle.

item.title: Save The Castle: Update
item.description: The #savethecastle now has a website where you can keep in touch with the latest news.

item.title: Save The Castle
item.description:
Sad news has reached us. Our beloved Castle has just learned that a planning application has been sent to the council to knock it down and build apartments and a parking lot!

Go to wandsworth.gov.uk find a planning application and search for application number 2012/0229 to protest and keep track of the application.

item.title: Afternoon tea and ice skating
item.description: Today we went to have afternoon tea at the Washington Mayfair Hotel. Nice place and lovely atmosphere but I don’t think I’d come here again. Staff are friendly enough and at £25 a head for afternoon tea including a cocktail, the price isn’t bad. What spoiled it is the poor quality of the food and drinks. Tasted like it was pre packed and not made up fresh.

item.title: Masa Bristol - A great sushi place
item.description:
Happy New Year to all.

To celebrate, I’ve just eaten some excellent sushi at Masa in Bristol. Really nice and cozy and some wonderful cooking too!

item.title: Christmas holidays
item.description:
Have a good end to the year!

I’m in Ireland for the holidays making the most of my time off and the awesome hospitality at my girlfriends home.

Next year I am planning to get away at least once for a spot of snowboarding, buy a tv, take first steps into the world of I.T. Architecture, save up loads for a house and possibly brew my own beer. Whether I complete all of that is another story. See you all in the new year!

item.title: Cooking simple but hearty food
item.description:
I am following this food blog.

http://www.rkfood.blogspot.com/

Made a lovely recipe from it. No fuss no hassle. Good food. See if you can spot which one I made.

item.title: Crazy microwave
item.description: We have a crazy microwave at home that seems to count in the hundreds?! Not regular time but in hundreds. Cool you may think, the microwave is compliant with metric time! But no. Only the first hundred is observed after that it reverts to regular sixty second time.

item.title: I'm With You
item.description: I have the new Red Hot Chili Peppers album, I’m With You, and I’ve been listening to it on repeat for a week.

item.title: The Tariq Effect
item.description: Picture the scenario: You’re working on a high profile account work millions of Pounds in charge of a team of skilled developers all working to create the client’s next generation system. You have to interface with different teams throughout the project as is the nature with any large delivery project. Teams such as Business Analyst team, Rules team, Business Process Management team and a CRM team. All teams are working harmoniously with each other. Sure they have issues and blockers and sometimes your code needs a rethink but nothing out of the ordinary considering the project is of a fair size.

item.title: Wet Berlin
item.description: Berlin is lovely, but very wet. I blame the best man for organising some of the worst weather for a stag do ever.

item.title: Off to Berlin
item.description: I’m off to Berlin for the weekend for a good friend’s stag do.

item.title: Unit Testing
item.description:
Hello again!

I wanted to share with everyone the importance of the V model approach to testing. I’ve seen a lot of places (and people) who don’t understand this approach so this is my way of explaining it.

item.title: New blog name
item.description: I’m thinking about changing the name of my blog. Big move - maybe? But I’m pretty sure I can do that with the Blogger engine. I guess the only thing to worry about is updating people’s links. But no one reads this blog anyway so what’s the problem?

item.title: Music in the rain
item.description: Band practice kicks off again with a trip back to Beit Hall at Imperial College.

item.title: fffffffuuuuuuuuuuuu
item.description:
Loving this right now

RageMaker

item.title: Recipe for Smoked Salmon Tagliatelle
item.description:
Ingredients:

Smoked Salmon
Fresh tagliatelle
Single cream
Yummy cheese
Salt
Pepper
Herby stuff (dill, etc)
Egg
Paprika (optional)
Olive oil
Butter
White wine (optional)

Instructions:

  1. Crack egg into bowl and whisk
  2. Put tagliatelle into pot and simmer with hot water with olive oil
  3. Add cream to egg whisk and whisk
  4. Add the herby stuff to the egg and cream whisk and whisk
  5. Add salt and pepper to the egg, cream and herby whisk and whisk
  6. Grate cheese into the egg, cream, herby stuff, salt and pepper whisk and whisk
  7. Flake salmon into the egg, cream, herby stuff, salt, pepper and cheese whisk and whisk

Drain tagliatelle and mix with the bowl of whisk with butter and a glass of white wine.

Enjoy!

item.title: New tracks
item.description: Today is the first time in over a month and a bit that our, yet to be named, band will be practising together!

item.title: Snowboarding in Oslo
item.description:
Hello!

I’m snowboarding in Oslo for three days and I can say it is a lovely place. The scenery is amazing and the snow is awesome too. The air is a special Nordic fresh that fills your lungs with goodness each time you take a breath.

item.title: Better ways to test OSB services
item.description: This week I have been trying to find out more about how to unit test Oracle Service Bus services in a way that lends itself to automation and continuous integration.

item.title: Need to blog more
item.description: I need to blog more. And with an aim to do that I have downloaded the new Google Blogger client for Android. Stay tuned for some more blogs!

item.title: My thoughts with Cyanogen 6.1 on the HTC Desire
item.description: For about a month I have been trying out the 6.1 release of the popular cyanogen custom roms on my HTC Desire. My first thoughts were that it was incredible. The amazing ease with which you could modify the settings to the n th degree was an eye opening experience. I loved how anything I wanted could be customised. And I mean anything. From the colour of the battery icon to the tool bar to the fact that you could make the phone vibrate slightly when your call has been answered. Or the way that you can change how your Launcher looks and how if you want you can have root access to the file system.

item.title: December quick check in
item.description: Haven’t been blogging here for a while because I much prefer Twitter…. sorry. Will try and summarise stuff every now and again though.

item.title: November madness
item.description:
Wow

Lots to write about but really can’t be bothered at the moment.

item.title: Update on the droid picture project
item.description: Hmm things not going well for the project.

item.title: A quick poem
item.description:
Really quick now:

I was chatting to my friend over the email communicator and I typed out this poem whilst sitting on the tube. Looking back I think it is kinda cool.

Oddly shaped electrical thing,
With all the wires ’n cables you bring.
You’re my magnificent window to a world unseen,
From robots and stars to mars’ moon beams.
Ever I ponder I will turn to you.
Ever I ponder you show me what’s true.
For you are the one that connects my life,
Across oceans and seas to the people I like.
I know not what I’ll do should this come to an end,
You’re my oddly shaped electrical friend.

Hope you like it!

item.title: Update on the droid picture project
item.description: It’s going well. I still can’t program for shit though. I need to learn how to make more effective use of the android documentation though.

item.title: Time to put my powers to good use
item.description: But first….. I need to get some powers.

item.title: Getting involved in testing Firefox 4
item.description:
Wow!! I really like the open source community!

And this is why……….. http://quality.mozilla.org/

item.title: Not much been going down
item.description: What have I been doing recently?

item.title: Election night
item.description:
I sort of agree with Puff Daddy’s Vote Or Die campaign. I also agree with voter apathy. Strange contradictions?

I don’t believe so.

item.title: Ash
item.description: I’m going to see Ash at the Electric Ballroom tonight. I’ve been a big fan since the Free All Angels album but sadly haven’t seen them yet.

item.title: Lazy bank holidays
item.description: Today was a pretty lazy day. And that is a good thing.

item.title: One day till Ubuntu goodness!
item.description: Lucid Lynx Ubuntu 10.04 is the new Ubuntu due out in a day.

item.title: What have i been doing?
item.description:
Well….

I have one of these! And boy is it sweet.

item.title: I have Android
item.description: I have the new Android phoned this is my first post on it!

item.title: Digital economy bill
item.description:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_Economy_Bill

Read up on that.

item.title: Snowboarding
item.description: I leave for France on Saturday for some snowboarding action. I can’t wait!

item.title: Where is Sin City book 2?
item.description: I can’t find Sin City Book 2 aaaaaaaaaanywhere? Is it out of print? Does it even exist?

item.title: Fully back on the 64
item.description: A while back I heaped praise on the Ubuntuzilla project for providing me a way to watch all Flash content without the need to wrap 32 bit plugins. This was extremely useful to me but I knew that it would always be a temporary thing.

item.title: B&W photos online
item.description:
I told you I’d get something up.

I hope this works! Click on the image to enjoy.

item.title: Photos from me
item.description:
As promised. Stuff I’ve been doing:

I know it is only one. I will try and upload some more later

item.title: Better flash and stuff...
item.description: Not much happening with me at the moment. I went to an art gallery and took some photos. If they come out nicely I shall post some of them!

item.title: What film scanner
item.description: I am in need of a film scanner to scan my film negatives.

item.title: Checking out google chrome on linux
item.description:
Considering it is a beta still, it is pretty good. I have to reset up my plugins and the extensions aren’t as good as they are on Firefox but blimey is it quick.

The one thing I’d like to see from Firefox now is speed. Speed to start and speed to roam.

item.title: First post of the decade!
item.description:
Wow! First post of the decade, blah blah blah blah how cool etc etc.

Right now this is mostly for my amusement but may be useful to someone else too.

item.title: Work laptop upgraded to Karmic
item.description: I upgraded my work laptop to the IBM Ubuntu reeeeeemix. I’m very impressed with the ease Ubuntu can be customised and how well that works with installations/ upgrades. Fantastic stuff.

item.title: Rage Against The Machine number 1
item.description: The best thing to happen all month.

item.title: Bash command craziness!
item.description: I had the following problem yesterday and it has taken me about 24 hours to work it out. The problem is as follows.

item.title: Developing for Android
item.description:
I thought I’d develop an application for the Android Market.

I hear it is pretty similar to java which is something I’d like to learn. I’ll post my source if I make something that actually works!

http://developer.android.com/index.html

item.title: Today's diet
item.description:
Today I mostly had:

Breakfast: 2 slices of wholemeal toast = 166 C
Lunch: Kids portion of porridge = 180 C
Drinks: 3 cups of black coffee = 16.2 C
Dinner: Lots of boiled vegetables and 3 roast chicken breasts = 1400 C

Total = 1762 C
Recommended daily allowance = 2500 C
Difference = 738 C

item.title: Calorie counting madness
item.description: On with this new diet and here are my results so far…..

item.title: New diet
item.description: Right back from holiday and watching South Park. Holiday was amazing but now I need to detox. I’m going to try and start a new diet which is as follows.

item.title: In Thailand
item.description:
I’m in Thailand for a two week holiday! Hurrah! Its been very good so far. Very relaxing and quite lively.

Unfortunately today has been rained off, but I don’t mind so much it forces me to detox from all the gin. Hmmmmm gin.

item.title: Beta testing karma
item.description:
I’ve not checked in for a long time but let me briefly let you know what I’ve been up to.

I’m testing the new Ubuntu release of Karmic Koala. It is in beta now and should be released at the end of October. Unfortunately I’m away on holiday in Thailand when it comes out so I wanted to have a little play around before I went. And I’ve already raised three bug reports on Launchpad already. Minor difficulties but overall it is an improvement.

item.title: Why 64?
item.description: A combination of a 64 bit build of Ubuntu, 9.04, 64 bit flash and an absurd naming of Firefox 3.5 have driven me to install a 32 build of Firefox onto my 64 bit machine. It means I can run flash 32 natively and without glitches and that my internet bank now recognises Firefox 3.5 instead of Shiretoko which it blocks access to.

item.title: Great British beer Festival
item.description: Today I have taken a whole day off work to drink with the west country folk in their hovel of ale and cider and country folk music. Big beers, even bigger girls and about a thousand drunkards. Love it.

item.title: Staying late for business
item.description: Staying in hotels ain’t all its cracked up to be.

item.title: Kensington Roof Gardens
item.description:
Friday saw us visit the Kensington Roof Gardens for a friends birthday. Fantastic place as usual. I think we made the right call by getting completely wasted before we went in because drinks were fantastically expensive.

I don’t remember how we all got home such was the brilliance of the night. I’m a bit out of it today though, as you can probably tell.

item.title: Life Beyond Measure
item.description: I’ve just finished reading my first book in a year! For the last year I have been encaptivated and excited in a child like trance by comics and graphic novel that I’ve come to love and yearn to dive deeper into a world of geekdom.

item.title: Chasers' War On Everything
item.description:
Chasers’ War On Everything

Comedy from Australia. Comes highly recommended. By me!

item.title: The Hangover
item.description: Four days, four films. Finally a good one.

item.title: Bruno
item.description: Today I went to see the new Sacha Baron Cohen film, Bruno. Being a massive fan of Ali G and Borat, I had high hopes for this one. Sadly it was disappointing on all levels.

item.title: Public Enemy
item.description: I’ve just seen Public Enemies with Johnny Depp, Christian Bale and that French chick Marion Cotillard. So so film. Nothing to shout about. In typical Michael Mann style it takes way too long to build up to a rather disappointing conclusion which was made worse by the fact that you already know that the crook was going to get caught.

item.title: Nine Inch Nails and Jane's Addiction
item.description: Wednesday was fantastic! I saw two childhood favourite bands, Nine Inch Nails (for the first time) and Jane’s Addiction (for the second time but the first with the original line up).

item.title: No new posts in a while
item.description:
So what have I been doing since I last posted here?

Not much. Work sleep work sleep work sleep work. That’s about it.

item.title: Overworked and underpaid
item.description: What the title says. Not impressed. I am finding myself falling asleep on the train. And not just because I am tired, but tactically now. I don’t get enough sleep so I use the train time to fall asleep.

item.title: Overtime shopping
item.description:
Sat at home working. Overtime. Double pay rate mind you.

Waiting for shopping to be delivered.

item.title: Comics Youtube and sun
item.description:
Today was kind of weird. I spent the whole day indoors but sun bathing sitting up against the window. Got about 3 good hours of just lazing about next to the window topless watching Xmen evolution on youtube. Marvel have put them all up to watch for free.

I’m still watching them now. On to season 2. I’ve made my self some shandy and am currently sipping it watching the rest of the season.

item.title: Training in the wilderness
item.description: This is the country house where I am being trained to sell to clients by structuring and developing my sales pitch.

item.title: Specials lost my phone :(
item.description:
Last Saturday I went to see one of the all time most awesome ska bands in the world ever…. The Specials!!

It was an amazing gig at Brixton Acedemy where they pretty much played all their best hits. I loved it when Rich Girl was played since that is my favourite song!

However……. because of all the skanking my phone must have fallen out of my pocket and I am now not the proud owner of a Sony Ericcson W350i. It is horrible! Eeeeew! I have to put up with it until March when I can leave my contract and get an Android phone. Good news is by then they will be even better than the Google phone 2.

item.title: Linux to the rescue
item.description:
My flat mate’s VISTA decided to corrupt itself. Rather than telling her to bin it and get a new one I used an Ubuntu live CD to view the hard drive and burn a back up onto DVDs.

And I did that for free. The only expense was a blank CD for the boot disc and a few blank DVDs to back up to.

How much would that have cost the MiCroShit way? Don’t even try to guess…….

item.title: Crazy impulse buy
item.description:
I just bought a media centre with a 1TB HDD and an external dvd usb player today.

How could I not? It was reduced by a penny!

item.title: Wolverine
item.description: On Sunday I saw the new X-Men Origins Wolverine.

item.title: Ubuntu update one week on
item.description: After about a week or so of using the new Ubuntu release Jaunty, I’ll give my opinion on it.

item.title: Secret Cinema
item.description:
Idea is you pay for a cinema ticket for a screening that you have no clue as to what it is.

If you like film and you love the whole experience of being excited at what you see without hype spoiling it then this is for you.

item.title: Start of a crappy great week
item.description: So this week starting off crap. Rain, rain and more rain. Stuck in the office well past everyone else. District line suspended. More rain. FUCK!!

item.title: 2 days to go!!!
item.description:
Only two days to go for maximum geekiness!

Ubuntu 9.04 released on Thursday.

Only question now is K or U.

Ultimate GEEK!!

item.title: New Ubuntu 5 days away
item.description: The new Ubuntu is only 5 days away. I can’t wait. It has been a year since I started my trip down Linux lane and am enjoying it more and more.

item.title: Battle Of The Year
item.description: Here are some pictures from battle of the year. A break dancing competition that was held in Angel.

item.title: Gin gin gin gin gin
item.description: 7 days to go till a new version of Ubuntu is released!! That’s my geekness done.

item.title: The Inbetweeners
item.description:
This programme is awesome! Kind of like Peep Show with 6th formers. Exactly how I remember school. Us fucking about swearing quite casually and laughing at each other getting into trouble and screwing up. Hahahaha!

Season 2 on air now!

item.title: Channel 4 catch up available on Linux!!
item.description:
OMG!!

4OD is now available in streaming flash format!! Much like BBC iPlayer! This means a move away from crappy wmv formats and a step in the right direction for support for other OSs such as Linux and macs.

Go Channel 4!!

item.title: Start of the 4 day holiday weekend
item.description: So Britain is in the midst of a four day Easter holiday season. The roads are jam packed out of London, Hammersmith is burning down and I’ve done nothing all day but each fried chicken and watched television.

item.title: Revitalised by the gym
item.description: Go to the gym everyone. It feels good. Plus there are beautiful people there!

item.title: Mad jamz
item.description: Yesterday I jammed out super funky style with K and Ricky. We’re going to make it a weekly thing. K spits out some wicked rhymes. I’m on drums by the way. Jam session

item.title: Rampaaaage
item.description:
Last night I rampaged through London.

Tonight I will rest and catch up of many hours of TV.

item.title: Stereophonics at the Albert Hall
item.description: Thursday night saw me and my old lab partner go see Stereophonics at the Royal Albert Hall.

item.title: Lines and lines and lines and lines and lines and lines and lines and lines.....
item.description:
I can’t find myself on Google Street View.

Shocking! I swear I saw the car whilst walking about.

item.title: Friday Saturday un happies
item.description: Sway.

item.title: Sky at night
item.description: I’m looking at the sky.

item.title: Wasted at work
item.description:
I….

Am…..

So……

Wasted…..

item.title: Crashing around the tube
item.description: I crashed into 3 people on my tube journey today.

item.title: Some phone pics
item.description: Some pictures from my phone.

item.title: This is a blog
Super 8mm Madness
Rugby England France
